title: Smart Honeypot System for Analyzing Zero-Day Attacks
shortDesc: A honeypot simulating SSH, FTP, HTTP, Telnet and SMB services to capture and analyze attacker behavior in real time.
features: Simulates SSH, FTP, HTTP, Telnet and SMB services to capture attacker activity
Real-time dashboard for monitoring attacks, protocols, and threat statistics
IP reputation analysis, anomaly detection, and GeoIP tracking
Generates security reports in CSV, JSON and PDF formats
challenges: Building a real-time dashboard that could reflect live attacker sessions without blocking the honeypot services required moving event delivery onto WebSockets via Django Channels backed by Redis as the channel layer.
learnings: Gained hands-on experience with asynchronous Python (Django Channels), designing services that intentionally expose themselves as attack surfaces, and turning raw connection logs into structured threat intelligence.
futureImprovements: Add more emulated services, integrate a real threat-intel feed for IP reputation, and containerize the system with Docker for easier deployment.

title: PDF Malware Analysis Toolkit
shortDesc: A static analysis toolkit that inspects PDF files for embedded threats, malicious JavaScript, and indicators of compromise.
features: Static PDF malware analysis using PDFiD and peepdf
Extracts metadata, embedded JavaScript, and embedded objects
challenges: PDF files can hide malicious content across nested objects and obfuscated JavaScript streams, so correctly parsing the object structure without executing anything was the core challenge.
learnings: Learned how PDF malware typically hides payloads (auto-actions, embedded JS, obfuscated streams) and how to build a scoring system that reflects real risk rather than just flagging keywords.
futureImprovements: Integrate YARA rule updates automatically and add a sandboxed dynamic analysis mode for deeper inspection.

title: Process Monitor Agent
shortDesc: A lightweight Windows agent that watches running processes, flags suspicious behavior, and raises real-time alerts.
features: Continuous Windows process monitoring using psutil
challenges: Distinguishing genuinely suspicious process behavior from normal system noise required tuning detection heuristics to keep false positives low.
learnings: Built a practical understanding of endpoint monitoring fundamentals and how SOC tooling turns raw OS-level signals into actionable alerts.
futureImprovements: Add a central logging endpoint so multiple agents can report to one dashboard, and expand detection heuristics.
